page: Microsoft Azure A4m_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Av2 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, delivering 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 41 GB of local temporary storage and operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the A4m_v2 exhibits entry-level performance characteristics, placing in the bottom 10% across multiple testing categories. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks recorded scores of 350.4 and 1343.8, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 123.33 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 12.77 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads and 10.01 GB/sec for large-block writes. Geekbench testing yielded a single-core score of 336.0 and a multi-core score of 1106.0, while Passmark CPU and memory scores reached 1874.9 and 712.28.

Despite its lower computational throughput, the A4m_v2 offers a cost-effective option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU performance. The high memory capacity relative to its processing power makes it suitable for lightweight database operations, caching layers, and basic web serving. Redis SET operations achieved 1.84 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reached 1.45 GB/sec. It is best utilized for development, testing, or legacy applications where memory capacity is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Microsoft Azure A4m_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine within the Av2 family, designed for workloads requiring a higher memory-to-CPU ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes 41 GB of local storage and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance, with the CPU and memory subsystems scoring in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. For example, the Geekbench multi-core score is 1106.0, and the Passmark CPU score is 1874.9. This server is a cost-effective option for memory-reliant but computationally light applications, such as small database hosting, caching servers, and basic web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure A4m_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 41 GB of local storage, designed for entry-level workloads requiring higher memory capacity.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure A4m_v2 is a memory-intensive VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 41 GB of local storage for entry-level workloads.
tagline: An entry-level, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-reliant workloads and basic database hosting.

Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
